contractor: Concordia LLC, Pace Group LLC, A-1 Developers LLC
description: New Construction Planned Unit Development (P.U.D.) on a subdivided lot (originally 1721-35 St. Andrew St. and to be changed to 1725 St. Andrew St. Units A-P). Building 1 consists of seven (7) attached townhouse units (B-H) and is part of a Planned Unit Development project with 16 total units. Building 1 is one of four separate buildings on this P.U.D. project site. Attached Townhomes are 2 stories, with ground floor garage and driveway, and contain 2 bedrooms & 2 bathrooms each. Construction system consists of heavy timber structural frame, concrete grade beam foundation, CMU wall base, Structurally Insulated Panels (SIP), & Steel Stud walls, floors, and roof. Painted fiber cement board exterior wall finish and standing seam metal roof. Attached townhomes are sprinklered and each unit is separated by 1-hour rated demising walls.

description: CONSTRUCTION OF HOTEL (THE Z HOTEL)
Response to VCC Notice of Violation letter

This permit is for the renovation of an existing 41,703 square foot building owned by the American Red Cross. Project work is to include the conversion of the first-floor level office space into parking, partial replacement of existing first floor level storefront glazing with perforated metal screen, placement of two new exterior doors for vehicular access, replacement of existing chain link fencing with a new security fencing and gate, removal of existing ground level radio tower, and replacement of existing MEP systems throughout the facility. Painting of the existing exterior brick façade is under consideration. Additional renovations include a complete interior renovation to the 2nd, 3rd, and 4th floors. At the time, the 3rd and 4th floors would be “shell space” for a future tenant, storage, and mechanical rooms. The 2nd floor renovation would primarily be used for administrative office space and training rooms by the American Red Cross.
Remove remains of previously adjoining building, build new finished corner, miscellaneous tuck-pointing on parking lot wall of building.
Renovation of building. Architectural drawings attached.

description: The project consists of improvements to the existing artists’ carts storage area located at the ground level under Oscar Dunn Park across from Jackson Square along Decatur Street. Work includes the demolition and removal of a section of concrete wall, the removal of existing surface materials including sheets of plywood, removal of a top level of fill material and the removal of other miscellaneous items. New work includes the construction of a section of concrete wall, installation of new concrete pavement and curb, a gravel trench, installation of new steel picket fencing and new eyebolt connectors.
